players.name: Datadog
players.offerings: Full-stack observability: metrics, logs, traces, APM, synthetic monitoring, security
players.target_customers: Engineering teams at growth-stage and enterprise companies
players.differentiators: Best-in-class integrations (650+), $2.1B ARR, strong developer adoption flywheel

players.name: Grafana Labs
players.offerings: Open-source dashboards, Loki (logs), Tempo (traces), Mimir (metrics), Grafana Cloud
players.target_customers: Engineering teams preferring open-source, especially on Kubernetes

players.name: New Relic
players.offerings: All-in-one observability: APM, infrastructure monitoring, distributed tracing, errors, logs
players.differentiators: Consumption pricing model, strong legacy installed base, OTel-native support

players.name: Honeycomb
players.offerings: High-cardinality event-based observability optimised for distributed systems
players.target_customers: Platform engineering and SRE teams at tech-forward companies
players.differentiators: BubbleUp ML-powered root cause analysis, OpenTelemetry-native, strong developer love

players.name: Dynatrace
players.offerings: AI-powered full-stack observability with automatic dependency mapping (Davis AI)
players.differentiators: Auto-instrumentation, strong enterprise contracts, AIOps leadership

players.name: Elastic (Elastic Observability)
players.offerings: Search-powered logs, metrics, traces on Elasticsearch; integrated SIEM
players.target_customers: Teams with heavy log search and security requirements
players.differentiators: Unified Elastic Stack, strong open-source adoption, self-hosted option

startups.velocity_note: Active Seed-to-Series-B activity in 2023-2024, with OTel-native tooling being the dominant investment thesis.

market_scan.notes: TAM from MarketsandMarkets APM and Observability report (2024). CAGR from IDC Cloud Management forecast. Medium confidence — adjacent categories (APM, log management) slightly inflate TAM estimates.

judgement.summary: 6 incumbents identified. 5 startups ($93M known funding). TAM ~$4.1B. CAGR ~16.8%. Market opportunity supports entry.
